entries.content:
# Feature Guide: Executives

**What Starting Monday gives you - one page**
**Platform entry:** startingmonday.app/for-executives | /for-cio | /for-ciso | /for-cdo | /for-cpo | /for-coo | /for-vp-technology

---

## Core features

### Company Intelligence Scanner
Monitors every company you track - every 48 hours - for signals that precede a senior search: executive departures, funding events, 8-K filings, leadership restructuring, and career page activity. When signals cluster into a pattern, the platform names the opportunity and alerts you before the role is formalized. This is the window that matters at the C-suite level.

### Daily Morning Briefing
Each morning you receive a digest of: new signals on tracked companies, pending follow-up actions, and your pipeline status. Installs the daily operating discipline that keeps a search active even when you are fully employed. Delivered via email and in-app.

### Active Pipeline Command Center
A single view of every company you are tracking: last contact, next action, signal freshness, and pipeline stage. Surfaces conversations that are going stale, follow-ups that are overdue, and timing windows that are narrowing. Acts as your CRM without requiring you to maintain it manually.

### Prep Brief Generator
Before any coffee chat, intro call, or interview, generate a role-specific prep brief. The brief includes: company context, likely mandate framing, probable interview questions for your target seat, suggested talking points, and positioning guidance. Takes 5 minutes to generate; replaces 2-3 hours of manual research.

### Persona Routes (role-specific guidance)
Choose the executive persona that matches your target seat: CIO/CTO, CISO, CDO, CPO, COO, VP Technology, or general C-suite. Each route surfaces role-specific search framing, interview arc patterns, and board-level positioning guidance calibrated to your target title.

### Active vs. Intelligence Mode
- **Active search:** Full daily cadence, all signals, pipeline tracking, outreach prompts. For executives running an intentional campaign.
- **Intelligence mode:** Reduced cadence, ambient signal awareness, no outreach prompts. For executives who want market intelligence without an active campaign footprint.

### Coach Visibility Controls
If you work with an executive coach, grant them read-only access to your pipeline and prep quality. You control exactly what they see. Your coach cannot see anything you have not explicitly shared, and nothing is visible to your current employer.

### Outreach Tracking
Log every message you send: introduction request, referral follow-up, coffee chat. The platform tracks response rates, time-to-reply, and follow-through gaps. Surfaces the conversations most worth a second touch.

### What-Changed Summary
Before any follow-up conversation, view a what-changed summary: what has happened with that company since your last interaction, new signals, team changes, and relevant news. Walk into every conversation more current than the person you are meeting.

### Interview Arc Tracker
For active interview processes, track each round: prep brief completed, confidence score, key themes from prior rounds, questions to ask, and next steps. Keeps your interview campaign organized and your preparation quality consistent across rounds.

---

## Privacy and control

| Setting | Default | You control |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Employer visibility | None | Cannot be changed - never visible |
| Recruiter visibility | None | You grant access per recruiter |
| Coach visibility | None | You grant read-only or full access |
| Profile discoverability | Off | You turn on when ready |

---

## Persona-specific features

| Persona | Additional features unlocked |
|---------|------------------------------|
| CIO / CTO | Technology mandate framing, CTO succession signal detection |
| CISO | Security leadership market timing, regulatory trigger monitoring |
| CDO / Chief Data Officer | Data mandate governance context, AI leadership positioning |
| CPO | Product leadership compensation benchmarks, board-level product framing |
| COO | Operational scope expansion signals, PE portfolio COO search patterns |
| VP Technology | VP-to-C-suite upgrade arc, technology leadership progression framing |

---

## Pricing

Free trial available - no credit card required. See startingmonday.app/pricing for paid plan details.

entries.content:
What Starting Monday gives coaches - one page
Platform entry: startingmonday.app/for-coaches | /partners | /for-coaches/search-affiliate

## Core features

### Pre-Session Snapshot
Before each client session, review one screen that summarizes what changed since the last conversation: new signals, pipeline movement, confidence trend, commitments kept or missed, and one recommended talking point for the upcoming session. In early 2026 pilots, coaches reported less context-rebuild time (directional, qualitative); results vary by workflow and adoption quality.

### Client Portfolio Dashboard
A portfolio-level view of all active clients: active vs drifting status, confidence trend, pipeline velocity, prep quality score, and last session date. It is designed to highlight which clients need intervention first.

### Client Visibility Controls (client-controlled)
Clients set what coaches can see. Options include read-only signals, pipeline access, full prep brief access, or no access. Coaches cannot view anything that has not been granted.

### Session Commitment Tracker
Log commitments made in each session. The tracker surfaces follow-through, at-risk commitments, and overdue actions before the next call.

### Coach Partner Program
Earn 20% recurring commission on active client referrals. Enrollment is free at startingmonday.app/partners and includes a referral link, onboarding materials, and messaging templates.

### Practice Model Paths
Choose the coach persona route that matches how you operate:
- Independent coach (solo practice): 1-15 clients, lightweight portfolio view, referral-only commission model
- Boutique firm coach: Team-based operations, shared client visibility settings, standardized scorecards
- Enterprise-sponsored coach cohort: Cohort governance, sponsor-ready reporting, program-level risk queue
- Search-affiliate coach: search-adjacent workflow with trust-boundary framing and interview-arc support

### 30-Day Pilot Scorecard
A structured pass/fail pilot framework for evaluating the platform with 2-3 clients. Metrics include context-rebuild time, prep quality, between-session momentum, and client confidence trend.

### Weekly Activity Digest
Each week, receive a portfolio summary: who is active, who is drifting, newly surfaced signals, and the client most at risk of losing momentum.

### Trust Pack
Documentation for coaches and firms evaluating broader rollout: methodology notes, claims policy, pilot evidence with denominator disclosures, security posture, and diligence path. See startingmonday.app/for-coaches/trust-pack.

### Boutique Firm Tools (Boutique plan and above)
- Shared client views across the coaching team
- Standardized session scorecard template configurable per firm
- Coach performance benchmarking (aggregate and anonymized)
- Sponsor-ready cohort reporting for enterprise-sponsored programs

### Search-Affiliate Workflow
For coaches working alongside retained search firms: trust-boundary documentation, interview-arc visibility, and proof assets for partner communication.

## What coaches see vs. what clients control

| Feature | Client controls | Coach sees |
|---------|-----------------|------------|
| Target company list | Client adds/removes | Visible if client grants |
| Signal feed | Client's signals only | Read-only if granted |
| Pipeline status | Client manages | Summary view if granted |
| Prep briefs | Client generates | If client shares |
| Confidence scores | Auto-generated | Always visible to coach |
| Commitment tracker | Set in session | Visible to coach |

## Session workflow

| Touchpoint | What to do | Time required |
|-----------|------------|---------------|
| Day before session | Open pre-session snapshot | 5 min |
| Start of session | Reference what-changed summary | 2 min |
| During session | Log commitments in real time | 1-2 min |
| After session | Update next-action and risk markers | 2 min |
| Weekly | Review portfolio risk queue | 10 min |

## Partner program economics

- Enrollment: Free
- Commission: 20% recurring on active referral subscriptions
- Payout: Monthly, with referral tracking dashboard
- No minimum: earn on every referral, no threshold required
- Apply: startingmonday.app/partners

Free trial available for referred clients. Program terms and payout schedule are available in the partner dashboard.

entries.title: Feature Guide: Outplacement Firms

entries.lastLine: Custom pricing available for programs above 300 participants. Contact your account lead.
entries.lastLineClipped: Custom pricing available for programs above 300 participants. Contact your account lead.
entries.content:
# Feature Guide: Outplacement Firms

**What Starting Monday gives outplacement and transition firms - one page**
**Platform entry:** startingmonday.app/for-outplacement | /for-outplacement/operating-scorecard | /for-outplacement/runbook | /for-outplacement/trust-pack

---

## Core features

### Cohort Operating Dashboard
Program-level visibility across all active cohorts: activation rate, signal-response rate, prep quality, confidence trend, and milestone completion. Identifies which cohorts are on track and which need intervention before weekly governance reviews. Available to practice leaders and program operations directors.

### Participant Operating Layer
Each participant in your program gets a private, structured daily cadence:
- Morning briefing with new signals and pending actions
- Company intelligence scanner monitoring their target list every 48 hours
- Pipeline command center for tracking conversations and follow-through
- Prep brief generator for interview and networking preparation

This runs between counselor sessions, not instead of them.

### Counselor Pre-Session Snapshot
Before each counseling session, the counselor views a one-screen summary: what changed since last session, current pipeline status, confidence score trend, recent signal activity, and one recommended coaching focus. Reduces context-rebuild time from 20-30 minutes to 5-12 minutes. Counselors spend 65-80% of session time on strategy vs. the current 45-55%.

### Milestone Tracking and Cohort Milestones
Track each participant through the program milestones:
1. Intake complete (account set up, target list baseline established)
2. Prep ready (first prep brief generated and reviewed)
3. First outreach sent (first signal-driven outreach logged)
4. Follow-up closure (first follow-up conversation completed)

Real-time milestone completion rates visible at the cohort and participant level.

### Sponsor Export and Reporting
Generate sponsor-ready reports on demand:
- Cohort activation and utilization rate
- Milestone completion rate with methodology notes
- Cadence adherence rate (how consistently participants are taking daily actions)
- Confidence trend and placement outcome summary
- KPI definitions and measurement methodology for procurement reviewers

Formatted for delivery to HR buyers, CFOs, and program sponsors.

### Weekly Governance Pack
A structured weekly review artifact for program leads: cohort pipeline review, stalled participant triage, intervention assignment list, and next-week plan. Ready to use in Monday operating meetings without additional preparation.

### Counselor Intervention Queue
A prioritized list of participants most at risk of going off-track: drifting pipeline, declining confidence, missed milestones, low signal-response rate. Surfaced automatically so counselors do not have to manually audit their client list to find who needs help.

### Operating Cadence Runbook
A step-by-step guide for counselors covering the recommended daily, pre-session, and post-session workflow. Includes: how to read the session snapshot, how to log commitments, how to use the weekly activity digest, and how to escalate a participant to the program lead. At startingmonday.app/for-outplacement/runbook.

### Cohort Variance and Benchmark Metrics
Compare cohorts against each other and against your own program baseline. Identify which cohort configurations (track, counselor ratio, session cadence) produce the best outcomes.

### 30-Day Pilot Scorecard
Structured pilot framework with four weekly milestones and clear success thresholds. At week 4, the scorecard outputs a pass/fail result tied to: week-1 activation rate, week-2 signal-response rate, week-3 prep quality, and overall placement velocity vs. baseline. Designed to make the rollout decision straightforward, not negotiable.

### Role-Based Access Controls
Assign the right access level to each team member:
- **Practice leader:** All cohorts, all reports, billing, firm-level benchmarks
- **Program operations director:** Cohort management, reporting, counselor oversight, no billing
- **Counselor lead:** Assigned cohorts, participant-level access, session snapshots
- **Procurement / legal reviewer:** Trust pack access, claims policy, methodology notes, security posture

### Trust Pack
Board-safe claims documentation for procurement and legal review: KPI definitions, measurement methodology, pilot evidence with denominator disclosures, data residency, security posture, and legal review path. Designed to satisfy enterprise procurement requirements. At startingmonday.app/for-outplacement/trust-pack.

---

## Pilot evidence (early 2026 observations, qualitative and directional)

| Metric | Result | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Session context-rebuild time reduction | 15-25 min saved per session | Counselor self-report |

Full methodology available in the trust pack.

---

## Program tiers

| Tier | Participants | Best for |
|------|-------------|---------|
| Solo | Up to 10 | Boutique transition specialists |
| Boutique | Up to 75 | Regional outplacement providers |
| Outplacement | Up to 300 | National and enterprise programs |

Custom pricing available for programs above 300 participants. Contact your account lead.

entries.content:
# Feature Guide: Executive Search Firms

**What Starting Monday gives retained search firms - one page**
**Platform entry:** startingmonday.app/search-firms | /search-firms/sample-cfo-brief

---

## Core features

### Role-Specific Prep Brief Generator
Generate a structured interview preparation brief for any candidate on any active mandate. The brief includes: target company context, likely mandate framing, probable hiring committee questions for the specific seat, candidate positioning guidance, and suggested talking points. Replaces 2-3 hours of manual research per candidate per round. See a live example at startingmonday.app/search-firms/sample-cfo-brief.

### Candidate Readiness Scoring
Before each interview round, the platform scores candidate readiness across four dimensions: company knowledge depth, role mandate understanding, positioning narrative strength, and executive presence indicators. Gives delivery leads an objective basis for "candidate is ready" vs. "candidate needs more prep."

### Mandate Command Center
Track each active retained search: candidate pipeline, interview round statuses, prep brief completion rates, and placement timeline. Surfaces mandates with preparation gaps (candidates advancing without completed prep briefs) and timeline risks.

### Kickoff Quality Checklist
Before the first client kickoff call on any new mandate, use the structured checklist: position framing, success criteria definition, hiring committee dynamics, search timeline constraints, and competitive intelligence. Ensures consistent kickoff quality across the team regardless of which consultant runs the meeting.

### Candidate Activity Visibility
When a candidate is invited to use the platform, delivery leads can see: prep brief review status, confidence trend, activity since last touchpoint, and any questions or concerns the candidate has flagged. Reduces the "how is the candidate feeling about this process?" uncertainty before round handoffs.

### Interview Arc Tracker
Track the full arc of each candidate's interview process: which rounds they have completed, key themes from each round, their prep approach for each stage, and what questions to expect next. Useful for delivery leads managing multiple candidates across multiple rounds simultaneously.

### Company Intelligence Feed
For any company in your active mandate list, monitor ongoing: executive departures, board changes, funding events, earnings signals, and strategic announcements. Surfaces new context between client calls so consultants arrive current at every touchpoint.

### Candidate Invitation Flow
Send candidates a personalized invitation with a single click. The invitation is written from the candidate's perspective (what they get, how it helps them, what privacy protections apply). No login required for candidates to review their first brief. Removes the explanation burden from the search professional.

### Search Team Access Roles
Control access for your entire search team:
- **Partner / firm lead:** Firm-wide view, all mandates, team performance benchmarks, billing
- **Principal / delivery lead:** Assigned mandates, full candidate access, prep quality oversight
- **Candidate success owner:** Candidate-level access, prep brief generation, readiness scoring

### Sample Brief Library
A library of sample prep briefs by role type: CFO, CTO, CHRO, CMO, COO, General Counsel, and others. Use as reference, training material for new consultants, or to demonstrate output quality to prospective clients. Available at startingmonday.app/search-firms/sample-cfo-brief.

### Client-Facing Progress Update
Generate a candidate preparation progress update for the client hiring team: prep brief completion rates, readiness scores by round, and preparation quality benchmarks vs. your firm's standards. Demonstrates rigor and candidate stewardship in a format clients can share internally.

### Confidentiality Controls
- Candidate data is isolated per firm. No cross-firm visibility.
- Candidates see only their own brief and activity. No exposure to other candidates in the same search.
- Hiring client contacts see only the progress export you choose to share. No access to candidate raw prep data.

---

## Impact by search phase

| Phase | Feature used | Time saved | Quality improvement |
|-------|-------------|------------|---------------------|
| Mandate kickoff | Kickoff quality checklist | 30-45 min prep | Standardized across team |
| Candidate briefing | Prep brief generator | 2-3 hours per candidate | Consistent role-specific depth |
| Interview rounds | Readiness scoring + arc tracker | 15-20 min per round | Objective readiness signal |
| Candidate management | Activity visibility | 1-2 check-in calls avoided | Proactive gap detection |
| Client reporting | Progress update export | 1 hour per update | Professional, data-backed |

---

## Candidate experience

When you invite a candidate to Starting Monday:
1. They receive a link to their personalized prep brief (no account required for first view)
2. They can create a free account to track their process, use the daily briefing, and access the interview arc tracker
3. You see their preparation activity and confidence level before each round
4. Their data is private to them and your firm - no other recruiter or employer can see it

---

## Firm pricing

Pricing is per mandate or per seat. Contact startingmonday.app/search-firms or your account lead for current firm pricing. Individual candidate accounts are free.

entries.content:
# Feature Guide: White-Label Partners

**What Starting Monday's white-label offering gives partner firms - one page**
**Platform entry:** Dashboard > Settings > White Label | Contact your account lead

---

## Core features

### Full Brand Customization
Your participants, counselors, and sponsors interact entirely with your brand. Configure:
- **Logo:** Appears on the dashboard, all emails, and exported reports
- **Brand name:** Replaces all "Starting Monday" references in the participant experience
- **Primary color:** Navigation, headers, and primary UI elements
- **Accent color:** CTAs, highlights, and key action buttons
- **Support email:** All participant support routing goes to your address

Changes apply instantly with a live preview before you save.

### Program Track Selection
Choose the participant journey that matches your program type:
- **Executive Transition:** VP and C-suite placements. Milestones: Intake complete, Prep ready, First outreach sent, Offer recorded.
- **Professional Transition:** Manager, director, and IC placements. Milestones: Intake complete, Positioning ready, First outreach sent, Interview completed.

Custom milestone sets available on Boutique and Outplacement tiers.

### Multi-Cohort Operations Hub (Outplacement tier)
Manage multiple simultaneous cohorts from one dashboard: active cohorts, enrollment status, milestone completion rates, counselor assignments, and sponsor report queue. Designed for national and enterprise programs running several cohort cycles per quarter.

### Cohort Dashboard and Participant Management
- Create named cohorts with custom start dates and program tracks
- Assign counselors to cohorts with configurable client-to-counselor ratios
- Track individual participant progress through program milestones
- Triage drifting or at-risk participants before they become escalations

### Sponsor Report Export (Boutique and Outplacement tiers)
Generate fully branded sponsor reports on demand:
- Cohort activation and utilization rates
- Milestone completion rates with methodology disclosure
- Confidence trend and engagement cadence
- KPI definitions and measurement notes
- Formatted for HR buyer, CFO, and board-level review

All reports use your brand name and logo. No Starting Monday attribution required unless you choose to include it.

### Governance Pack (Outplacement tier)
A complete governance documentation set for enterprise program management:
- SLA reporting templates
- Executive readout frameworks
- Cohort risk and intervention tracking
- Weekly operating review artifacts
- Procurement and legal review documentation

### Partner Role Management
Assign granular access levels to your team:
- **Firm admin:** Full settings access, billing, all cohorts, brand configuration
- **Program manager:** Cohort management, reporting, counselor oversight, no billing
- **Counselor:** Assigned participants only, session snapshots, no admin access

Add or revoke roles at **Dashboard > Settings > Team > Partner Roles**. Audit log available for enterprise accounts.

### White-Label Email System
All participant-facing emails are sent from your support address with your branding:
- Welcome and activation emails
- Daily briefing digest (your brand, your colors)
- Session reminder and commitment follow-ups
- Weekly activity summary
- Milestone completion notifications

Participants never see an email from Starting Monday.

### Priority Support (Boutique and Outplacement tiers)
Dedicated support response SLA: 4-hour response during business hours for Boutique, 2-hour for Outplacement. Includes a named account lead for quarterly business reviews and pilot planning.

### Participant Operating Features (included in all tiers)
Every participant in your white-labeled environment has access to the full participant feature set:
- Company intelligence scanner (48-hour monitoring cycle)
- Daily morning briefing
- Pipeline command center
- Prep brief generator
- Interview arc tracker
- Coach visibility controls (if your program includes coaching)
- What-changed session summaries

All delivered under your brand.

### Billing and Seat Management
- Overage seats are billed automatically at your tier's per-seat rate
- Add seats mid-month and pay prorated
- Upgrade tiers immediately; downgrade takes effect next billing cycle
- Usage dashboard shows seat consumption vs. included seats in real time

---

## Tier comparison

| Feature | Solo ($299/mo) | Boutique ($1,500/mo) | Outplacement ($5,000/mo) |
|---------|---------------|---------------------|------------------------|
| Included participants | 10 | 75 | 300 |
| Overage per seat | $19 | $15 | $10 |
| Program tracks | 1 | 2 | 2 |
| Full branding | Lite (logo + name) | Full | Full |
| Cohort dashboard | Basic | Full | Multi-cohort hub |
| Sponsor report export | - | Yes | Yes |
| Governance pack | - | - | Yes |
| SLA reporting | - | - | Yes |
| Priority support | - | 4-hour SLA | 2-hour SLA |
| Custom milestones | - | Yes | Yes |
| Named account lead | - | - | Yes |

---

## What your participants experience

From signup through placement, participants see only your brand:
- Onboarding email from your address
- Dashboard with your logo and colors
- Prep briefs and reports with your firm name
- Support routing to your team
- No Starting Monday branding visible by default

---

## Getting started

White-label activation requires a Starting Monday account. If you are not yet set up, contact support@startingmonday.app or request access through your account lead. Basic configuration (branding, track, first cohort) is typically live within 1 business day.

entries.title: Quick-Start Guide: Executives
entries.summary: **Audience:** VP and C-suite leaders running an active or passive job search
entries.lastLine: Go to **startingmonday.app/signup** and start your free trial. The platform is useful within minutes of adding your first target company.
entries.lastLineClipped: Go to **startingmonday.app/signup** and start your free trial. The platform is useful within minutes of adding your first target company.
entries.content:
# Quick-Start Guide: Executives

**Audience:** VP and C-suite leaders running an active or passive job search
**Time to first value:** Under 30 minutes
**URL:** startingmonday.app/for-executives

---

## What Starting Monday does for you

Starting Monday gives you a private, signal-first operating layer so you can run a high-quality executive search before roles are ever posted - without exposing your intent to current employers or recruiters.

---

## Step 1 - Sign up and choose your path (5 min)

1. Go to **startingmonday.app/signup**
2. Create a free account (no credit card required)
3. Choose your executive mode:
   - **Active search** - you are actively looking and want a full operating cadence
   - **Intelligence** - you want signals and awareness without a visible campaign
4. Optionally pick your persona route (CIO, CISO, CDO, CPO, COO, VP Technology) for role-specific framing

> Tip: Not sure which persona? Start with Active search. You can refine later.

---

## Step 2 - Build your target company list (5-10 min)

1. Navigate to **Dashboard > Discover**
2. Start with suggested target companies (based on your role path), then add 10-20 companies you want to track
3. The scanner begins monitoring each company every 48 hours for:
   - Executive departures and leadership changes
   - Funding events and 8-K filings
   - Career page postings for roles you could fill
   - News and strategic signals

---

## Step 3 - Set up your daily briefing (2 min)

1. Go to **Dashboard > Settings > Notifications**
2. Turn on the daily morning briefing
3. Each morning you receive: new signals on tracked companies, pending follow-up actions, and your pipeline status

---

## Step 4 - Create your first prep brief (5 min)

1. Go to **Dashboard > Prep Briefs**
2. Select a company or role you are exploring
3. The platform generates a role-specific brief: company context, likely mandate, interview themes, and talking points
4. Use this before any coffee chat, intro call, or first-round interview

---

## Step 5 - Track your pipeline (ongoing)

1. Go to **Dashboard > Pipeline**
2. Log each outreach, conversation, and follow-up
3. The platform surfaces stalled conversations and timing gaps
4. Weekly momentum summary shows your velocity and gaps

---

## What you will see in the first 30 days

| Week | Expected milestone | Artifacts produced |
|------|-------------------|--------------------|
| Week 1 | Target list set, first signals arriving | Target company list (10-20 companies), persona route confirmed, initial narrative draft aligned to your target mandate |
| Week 2 | First signal-driven outreach sent | Personal brand statement (one-paragraph positioning), strategy brief (target role, target companies, timing rationale), first outreach messages drafted and sent |
| Week 3 | At least one prep brief used before a real conversation | Role-specific prep brief (company context, likely mandate, interview themes, objection handling), relationship map (warm contacts, cold targets, search firm connections to activate) |
| Week 4 | Pipeline has 3+ active threads | Pipeline summary (active conversations, stalled threads, next actions), relationship warm log (who you contacted, what changed, what is next), weekly momentum review |

---

## Your privacy guarantee

- **Employer visibility: none.** No signals go to your current employer.
- **Recruiter visibility: none by default.** You control who sees what.
- You can revoke any coach or partner access at any time.

**How we protect your data internally:**

- **Row-level security (RLS).** Every database query is scoped to your user ID at the database layer. No application code can accidentally return another user's data, even in the event of a bug.
- **Encrypted at rest and in transit.** All data is encrypted at rest in our database and encrypted in transit over TLS. We do not store plaintext credentials anywhere.
- **Minimal data collection.** We collect only what is needed to run your search: your target list, your pipeline state, and your prep brief history. We do not sell leads, we do not license your data to third parties, and we do not use your data to train models.
- **Access controls by role.** Coach and partner visibility is gated by explicit permission grants that you set. Internal team access is role-scoped and logged.
- **Permanent deletion.** You can permanently delete your account and all associated data from Settings at any time. Deletion is irreversible and removes your data from active storage within 30 days.
- **Security practices.** We follow OWASP secure development guidelines, enforce API authentication on every endpoint, and run dependency audits as part of our deployment pipeline.

---

## First action right now

Go to **startingmonday.app/signup** and start your free trial. The platform is useful within minutes of adding your first target company.

entries.lastLine: Go to **startingmonday.app/for-coaches** and request the preview. It takes 10 minutes and makes the value visible before any commitment.

entries.content:
# Quick-Start Guide: Executive Coaches

**Audience:** Independent coaches, boutique firm coaches, and enterprise-sponsored coach cohorts
**Time to first value:** Under 20 minutes (preview), 30 days (full pilot)
**URL:** startingmonday.app/for-coaches

---

## What Starting Monday does for you

Starting Monday gives you a private operating layer so clients arrive prepared, sessions stay strategic, and your coaching authority stays intact. You see signals, prep quality, and momentum - your client controls what you see.

---

## Step 1 - Run the coach preview (10 min)

1. Go to **startingmonday.app/for-coaches**
2. Click **Request the coach preview**
3. You will see: the client-side workflow, your visibility settings, the trust boundary, and directional observations from early 2026 pilots
4. Decide whether it fits your practice model before committing to anything

> No client data is required for the preview. This is a review-first motion by design.

---

## Step 2 - Choose your partner path

| Coach model | Best entry point |
|-------------|-----------------|
| Independent coach, 1-15 clients | Solo plan, refer clients via partner link |
| Boutique firm, 15-75 clients | Boutique plan, shared cohort dashboard |
| Enterprise-sponsored program | Outplacement plan, governance pack included |
| Search-adjacent coach | See /for-coaches/search-affiliate |

Apply at **startingmonday.app/partners** (free enrollment, 20% recurring commission on referrals).

---

## Step 3 - Set up your first client (5 min)

1. Log into the coach dashboard at **Dashboard > Coach > Clients**
2. Send your client an invitation link
3. Your client completes their own setup (target list, mode, preferences)
4. You receive a notification when they are active

---

## Step 4 - Use the pre-session snapshot

Before each client session:
1. Go to **Dashboard > Coach > Clients > [Client name]**
2. Open **Session Snapshot**
3. You see: what changed since last session, current pipeline status, momentum markers, confidence level, and one recommended talking point

This replaces the first 15-20 minutes of most sessions that are typically spent rebuilding context.

---

## Step 5 - Review the weekly activity summary

Each week:
1. Go to **Dashboard > Coach > Portfolio**
2. Review: active vs. drifting clients, stalled pipeline alerts, prep quality scores, confidence trend
3. Identify clients who need intervention before problems compound

---

## 30-day pilot scorecard

Run 2-3 clients through the platform for 30 days, then measure:

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Session context-rebuild time | Down from 20 min to under 10 min |
| Client preparation quality | Client arrives with pipeline status ready |
| Between-session momentum | Client takes at least 1 action per week |
| Client confidence trend | Stable or improving at week 4 |

Decide from evidence, not theory.

---

## Trust boundary (important)

- **Clients control your access.** They can grant read-only view of signals and pipeline, or full prep brief access.
- **No recruiter visibility.** Nothing crosses to recruiters without explicit client permission.
- **Your coaching authority is intact.** The platform handles execution; you own strategy.

---

## First action right now

Go to **startingmonday.app/for-coaches** and request the preview. It takes 10 minutes and makes the value visible before any commitment.

entries.title: Quick-Start Guide: Outplacement Firms
entries.summary: **Audience:** Practice leaders, program operations directors, counselors, and procurement reviewers at outplacement and transition firms
entries.lastLine: Go to **startingmonday.app/for-outplacement** and read the pilot outcomes. Then request a scoping call to define your pilot cohort.
entries.lastLineClipped: Go to **startingmonday.app/for-outplacement** and read the pilot outcomes. Then request a scoping call to define your pilot cohort.
entries.content:
# Quick-Start Guide: Outplacement Firms

**Audience:** Practice leaders, program operations directors, counselors, and procurement reviewers at outplacement and transition firms
**Time to first value:** 30-day pilot cohort
**URL:** startingmonday.app/for-outplacement

---

## What Starting Monday does for your firm

Starting Monday is the between-session operating layer for your counselors and participants. It improves cohort momentum, reduces counselor prep overhead, and gives your program-level leadership measurable placement outcomes to report to sponsors.

---

## Step 1 - Review the evidence and trust pack (15 min)

Before any internal decision:
1. Go to **startingmonday.app/for-outplacement**
2. Review the pilot observations: early cohorts showed faster first qualified outreach and stronger prep discipline (directional, methodology in trust pack)
3. Go to **startingmonday.app/for-outplacement/trust-pack** for methodology, KPI definitions, and claims policy
4. Share the trust pack with procurement and legal reviewers before advancing

---

## Step 2 - Define your pilot cohort (1-2 days)

Work with your Starting Monday account lead to define:

| Decision | Options |
|----------|---------|
| Cohort size | 10-30 executives recommended for initial pilot |
| Track | Executive Transition (VP+/C-suite) or Professional Transition (director and below) |
| Counselor count | 1 counselor per 10-12 participants recommended |
| Pilot duration | 30 days minimum for scorecard evidence |

---

## Step 3 - Pilot implementation schedule

| Week | Action | Owner |
|------|--------|-------|
| Week 0 | Kickoff and baseline setup - cohort defined, scorecard approved | Partner lead + Starting Monday |
| Week 1 | Activation sprint - participants set target list, first signal review | Counselors + participants |
| Week 2 | Signal-to-action - each participant logs at least one outreach action | Participants with counselor oversight |
| Week 3 | Prep quality checkpoint - at least one interview brief used | Counselors + participants |
| Week 4 | Scorecard review - decide on full program rollout | Program lead |

---

## Step 4 - Counselor workflow

Counselors use Starting Monday to:
- **Pre-session:** Read the session snapshot (what changed since last session, current pipeline, confidence score)
- **During session:** Spend 65-80% of session on strategy vs. the current 45-55%
- **Post-session:** Log commitments and risk markers
- **Weekly:** Review portfolio risk queue and identify participants needing intervention

> Counselors in the pilot cohort reduced context-rebuild time from 20-30 minutes to 5-12 minutes per session.

---

## Step 5 - Program-level reporting

The platform provides:
- Weekly cohort engagement dashboard: activation rate, signal-response rate, prep quality
- Sponsor-ready export: milestone completion rate, utilization, cadence adherence
- Risk triage: participants drifting, stalled pipelines, intervention queue

These reports go directly to your program lead and can be exported for sponsor reporting.

---

## Roles and responsibilities

| Role | Owns |
|------|------|
| Starting Monday platform | Signal detection, prep briefs, activity visibility, scorecard reporting |
| Counselors | Judgment, narrative coaching, prioritization, confidence support |
| Program lead | Cohort governance, adoption accountability, escalation decisions |

---

## Common questions

**"Our participants are overwhelmed and will not adopt another tool."**
Frame it as one morning decision list. Adoption improves significantly when counselors introduce it as "see what happened and decide one action" rather than "learn a new platform."

**"We already provide this in workshops."**
Starting Monday is not a replacement for counselors or workshops. It is the between-session layer that keeps execution visible between touchpoints.

---

## First action right now

Go to **startingmonday.app/for-outplacement** and read the pilot outcomes. Then request a scoping call to define your pilot cohort.

entries.title: Quick-Start Guide: Executive Search Firms

entries.content:
# Quick-Start Guide: Executive Search Firms

**Audience:** Partners, delivery leads, and candidate success owners at retained executive search firms
**Time to first value:** First candidate prep brief, within 1 hour
**URL:** startingmonday.app/search-firms

---

## What Starting Monday does for your firm

Starting Monday strengthens kickoff quality, shortlist velocity, and candidate readiness on retained mandates. Candidates arrive prepared at first-round interviews. Search professionals reduce prep admin and deliver a demonstrably better candidate experience.

---

## Step 1 - Review the firm-level value case (15 min)

1. Go to **startingmonday.app/search-firms**
2. Review how Starting Monday maps to your three key delivery stages:
   - **Kickoff:** mandate framing, candidate brief preparation, role-specific context
   - **Shortlist:** candidate readiness scores, preparation quality benchmarking
   - **Placement:** interview-arc tracking, post-offer follow-through
3. See a sample brief at **startingmonday.app/search-firms/sample-cfo-brief**

---

## Step 2 - Choose your entry point by role

| Role | Entry point |
|------|------------|
| Partner or firm lead | Mandate-level ROI and kickoff quality evidence |
| Delivery lead or principal | Candidate prep workflow and execution cadence |
| Candidate success owner | Prep brief generation and readiness scoring |

---

## Step 3 - Run your first candidate brief (30-45 min)

For any active retained search:
1. Request a firm account at **startingmonday.app/search-firms**
2. Add the mandate: target role, company, and stage of search
3. Add the candidate: seniority, background, key transitions
4. The platform generates:
   - Role-specific interview prep brief
   - Likely board or hiring committee questions
   - Mandate context and company background
   - Suggested talking points and positioning

---

## Step 4 - Set up the candidate

If you want the candidate to use the platform directly:
1. Send the candidate an invitation from the mandate dashboard
2. The candidate sets up their own account (free for candidates)
3. They access their prep brief, signals, and interview tracker
4. You can see their prep activity and confidence markers before each round

---

## Step 5 - Use the kickoff quality checklist

Before the first client kickoff call for any new mandate:
1. Go to **Dashboard > Mandates > [Mandate name] > Kickoff prep**
2. Use the structured checklist: position framing, success criteria, committee dynamics, search timeline
3. The platform surfaces relevant market intelligence and comparable role benchmarks

---

## What this improves

| Metric | Before | With Starting Monday |
|--------|--------|---------------------|
| Candidate first-round preparation | Ad hoc, variable quality | Structured brief with role-specific arc |
| Kickoff quality | Dependent on individual consultant | Standardized checklist with intelligence layer |
| Between-round follow-through | Manual check-ins | Tracked commitments and activity visibility |
| Search intelligence | Point-in-time research | Continuous signal monitoring on target companies |

---

## Trust and confidentiality

- Candidate data is visible only to the search firm and the candidate.
- No data crosses to competing firms or employers without explicit authorization.
- All mandate data is isolated per firm account.

---

## First action right now

Go to **startingmonday.app/search-firms/sample-cfo-brief** to see a real prep brief. Then request a firm account to run the same quality brief on your next active mandate.

entries.title: Quick-Start Guide: White-Label Partners
entries.summary: **Audience:** Outplacement firms, coaching practices, and transition service providers deploying Starting Monday under their own brand
entries.lastLine: If you do not yet have white-label access, contact your Starting Monday account lead or email support@startingmonday.app with your firm name and current cohort size. Setup typically completes within 1 business day.
entries.lastLineClipped: If you do not yet have white-label access, contact your Starting Monday account lead or email support@startingmonday.app with your firm name
entries.content:
# Quick-Start Guide: White-Label Partners

**Audience:** Outplacement firms, coaching practices, and transition service providers deploying Starting Monday under their own brand
**Time to first value:** Branded environment live within 1 business day
**URL:** Contact your Starting Monday account lead or request access via settings

---

## What the white-label offering is

White-label lets your firm deliver Starting Monday's operating platform under your brand name, colors, and domain. Your participants, counselors, and sponsors interact with your product - not Starting Monday's. You set the program structure; we power the execution.

---

## Step 1 - Choose your tier (15 min)

| Tier | Best for | Included participants | Price |
|------|----------|-----------------------|-------|
| Solo | Independent coaches, small practices | Up to 10 | $299/mo |
| Boutique | Boutique coaching firms, regional providers | Up to 75 | $1,500/mo |
| Outplacement | National/enterprise outplacement programs | Up to 300 | $5,000/mo |

Overage per additional participant: Solo $19, Boutique $15, Outplacement $10.

Choose a tier at **Dashboard > Settings > White Label**.

---

## Step 2 - Configure your branding (30 min)

In **Dashboard > Settings > White Label > Branding**:
1. Upload your logo (SVG or PNG, square format recommended)
2. Set your primary color (your firm's main brand color, as hex code)
3. Set your accent color (button and highlight color)
4. Set your brand name (appears in all client-facing emails and screens)
5. Set your support email (where participant support requests will route)

Changes apply immediately to your environment.

---

## Step 3 - Select your program track

| Track | Use when |
|-------|---------|
| Executive Transition | VP, C-suite, and senior director placements |
| Professional Transition | Manager, director, and individual-contributor placements |

Set at **Dashboard > Settings > White Label > Program Track**.

Each track has a default milestone set (Intake complete, Prep ready, First outreach sent, Offer recorded) that you can customize.

---

## Step 4 - Set up your first cohort

1. Go to **Dashboard > Programs > New cohort**
2. Name the cohort (e.g., "Q3 2026 Technology Leaders")
3. Set the cohort size and expected start date
4. Assign counselors to the cohort
5. Set the program track
6. Send participant invitations from the cohort dashboard

---

## Step 5 - Assign partner roles

Give your team the right access levels:
- **Firm admin:** full settings, billing, reports, all cohorts
- **Program manager:** cohort management, reporting, no billing
- **Counselor:** assigned client access, session snapshots, no admin

Assign roles at **Dashboard > Settings > Team > Partner Roles**.

---

## Step 6 - Export sponsor reports

When a sponsor (employer or HR buyer) needs a status update:
1. Go to **Dashboard > Cohorts > [Cohort name] > Reports**
2. Select **Sponsor Export**
3. The export includes: cohort activation rate, milestone completion, engagement cadence, and confidence trend
4. All data uses your brand name, not Starting Monday

---

## What your participants experience

Participants interact with your branded environment throughout:
- Onboarding emails from your support address
- Dashboard branded with your logo and colors
- Prep briefs and reports with your firm name
- No Starting Monday branding visible unless you choose to display it

---

## Tier upgrade path

Start at the tier that matches your current cohort size. Upgrades apply immediately mid-month (prorated). Downgrade takes effect at the next billing cycle.

---

## First action right now

If you do not yet have white-label access, contact your Starting Monday account lead or email support@startingmonday.app with your firm name and current cohort size. Setup typically completes within 1 business day.

entries.title: Onboarding and Time-to-Value Analysis
entries.summary: **Purpose:** Identify friction points in new-user onboarding for all five Starting Monday personas and recommend improvements that reduce time to first value.
entries.lastLine: This matrix should be reviewed monthly against actual activation and conversion metrics so priorities stay tied to measured outcomes.
entries.lastLineClipped: This matrix should be reviewed monthly against actual activation and conversion metrics so priorities stay tied to measured outcomes.
entries.content:
# Onboarding and Time-to-Value Analysis

**Purpose:** Identify friction points in new-user onboarding for all five Starting Monday personas and recommend improvements that reduce time to first value.
**Date:** 2026-06-17
**Owner:** Product

---

## Summary

Starting Monday serves five distinct buyer types with fundamentally different activation patterns. Executives need to see signals within minutes. Coaches need to trust the workflow before referring clients. Outplacement firms need a 30-day evidence window before committing. Search firms need a single compelling output artifact (the brief) before any other conversation. White-label partners need a configured, branded environment as fast as possible.

The largest opportunities are: (1) reducing the steps between signup and first meaningful signal for executives, (2) making the coach preview self-serve and instantly convincing, and (3) reducing the perceived pilot commitment for outplacement buyers.

---

## Persona-by-Persona Analysis

### 1. Executives

**Current path to first value:**
Signup > choose mode > build target list > configure notifications > first signal arrives (24-48 hours)

**Time to first value:** 24-48 hours to first signal (product mechanics)

**Friction points:**
- Target list setup requires the user to already know which companies they are targeting. First-time users often do not have a list ready, so they skip this step and the platform feels empty.
- The mode selection (Active vs Intelligence) is early in the flow but poorly framed. Users do not know what changes between the modes.
- The daily briefing is opt-in but is the highest-value activation habit. Many users never turn it on.
- The prep brief feature requires an interview or call context to feel useful. Until users have an actual conversation scheduled, prep briefs feel premature.

**Recommended improvements:**
1. **Starter company list:** On the target list setup screen, pre-suggest 10-15 companies based on the user's role type and most recent employer. Let users confirm or swap, so the list is never empty on day 1.
2. **Mode explainer:** A two-sentence tooltip on Active vs Intelligence, with a concrete example of how daily briefing volume differs, before the user makes the selection.
3. **Auto-enable daily briefing:** Make it opt-out rather than opt-in. Users who do not want it can turn it off; users who forgot to turn it on miss their highest-value touchpoint.
4. **"First signal" celebration moment:** When the first meaningful signal arrives (e.g., executive departure at a target company), surface a prominent notification with an explicit suggested action: "This company recently lost their CTO. This is often a 90-day window for a successor search. Here is a suggested outreach approach."
5. **Persona path pre-selection:** Add a 2-question intake during signup (current title, target title). Use this to pre-select the persona and surface role-specific guidance immediately.

---

### 2. Executive Coaches

**Current path to first value:**
Visit /for-coaches > request preview > preview call or async review > apply to partner program > refer first client > client activates > session snapshot available

**Time to first value:** 10 minutes (preview comprehension), 2-7 days (first client active), 2-4 weeks (session quality change visible)

**Friction points:**
- The coach preview currently requires a human-mediated step (a call or email thread). This creates drop-off between intent and action.
- The partner application form (at /partners) asks for practice details that feel like commitment before value is established. Coaches abandon at this step.
- Trust boundary framing is critical for coaches (they want to know the platform won't undermine their authority) but is buried in the trust pack rather than visible in the first 3 minutes.
- First client setup requires the coach to explain the platform to the client, which they cannot do confidently until they have used it themselves.

**Recommended improvements:**
1. **Self-serve interactive preview:** Replace the preview-request CTA with a 5-screen interactive flow that simulates what the coach sees before a session, including a sample session snapshot, a sample confidence trend chart, and a sample portfolio risk view. No call required.
2. **Lead with the trust boundary:** On /for-coaches, the very first content block below the headline should be the 3-sentence trust statement: what you see, what your client controls, and what recruiters never see.
3. **Lighter partner enrollment:** Change the partner application to a one-field form (email) that triggers an auto-responder with the referral link immediately. Capture more profile data in a follow-up email after trust is established.
4. **"Send to your first client" one-click:** Once a coach is enrolled, give them a single-click personalized invite link they can forward. The link explains the platform from the client's perspective, not the coach's.
5. **Week-2 check-in automation:** On day 14, send a coach a summary of their client's first two weeks of activity with one comparison: "Your client arrived prepared to 3 of 3 sessions vs. the 1-in-3 baseline without the platform." Make the ROI visible before they would otherwise notice it.

---

### 3. Outplacement Firms

**Current path to first value:**
Visit /for-outplacement > review features + pilot evidence > trust pack review > internal decision > pilot cohort scoped > counselors trained > week-1 activation > week-4 scorecard > rollout decision

**Time to first value:** 4-6 weeks (first scorecard evidence), 90 days (full rollout decision)

**Friction points:**
- The sales cycle is long by nature (B2B enterprise), but the internal champion often loses momentum waiting for procurement and legal review before the first pilot even starts.
- Counselors are skeptical because they fear added admin work. Their objection needs to be addressed before the pilot starts, not after.
- The pilot scorecard is clear (week 1-4 milestones) but the decision point at week 4 is not tied to a specific business outcome the practice leader cares about (placement rates, counselor capacity, sponsor renewal rates).
- Cohort setup requires the partner lead to manually configure everything. There is no guided onboarding wizard.

**Recommended improvements:**
1. **Lead with the counselor workflow, not the executive workflow.** On /for-outplacement, the first feature description should be: "What your counselors see before each session." This addresses the #1 adoption risk (counselor skepticism) before the internal champion has to.
2. **Self-service pilot calculator:** A simple 3-field form on the site (cohort size, current first-interview rate, sessions per counselor per week) that outputs: estimated improvement in first-interview rate, estimated hours saved per counselor, and revenue-per-placement impact. This gives the internal champion a business case to bring to the CFO.
3. **Guided cohort setup wizard:** Replace the open dashboard with a 5-step wizard for first-time cohort setup: cohort name, track selection, counselor assignment, participant invite, and scorecard baseline. One screen at a time with progress indicators.
4. **Counselor-first onboarding path:** Create a separate 20-minute onboarding flow specifically for counselors (not program leads). It should simulate one full session cycle using sample data before they interact with a real participant.
5. **Tie the week-4 decision to a specific metric:** Change the pilot scorecard CTA from "decide at week 4" to "at week 4, if activation is above 80% and first-interview rate is up by 15%+, here is the business case for full rollout." Eliminate the need for the champion to construct the argument themselves.

---

### 4. Executive Search Firms

**Current path to first value:**
Visit /search-firms > review firm value > see sample brief > request firm account > run first brief > invite first candidate

**Time to first value:** Under 1 hour (first brief generated), under 1 week (first candidate using platform)

**Friction points:**
- The sample CFO brief at /search-firms/sample-cfo-brief is compelling but static. Delivery leads want to know: "What would a brief look like for my actual current mandate?" They cannot test it without setting up an account first.
- Partner (firm leader) and delivery lead (consultant) have very different needs but land on the same page. Partners care about firm-level differentiation; delivery leads care about saving prep time on the next candidate call.
- Candidate invitation requires the search professional to explain the platform to the candidate. This adds a step and shifts the communication burden to the firm.

**Recommended improvements:**
1. **Try-before-you-commit brief generator:** Add a single-page tool at /search-firms/try that lets a visitor input role type, target company, and candidate background and receive a sample brief without an account. This proves the output quality in 3 minutes and removes the account-creation barrier for initial evaluation.
2. **Split landing page CTAs by role:** Add two explicit entry points on /search-firms: "I am a partner evaluating this for our firm" (leads to ROI evidence and comparison table) and "I have a search right now and want a better brief" (leads directly to account creation and brief generation).
3. **Candidate invitation template:** Provide a copy-paste email template that a search professional can send to a candidate, written from the candidate's perspective (what they get, why it helps them, no login friction). Removes the explanation burden from the firm.
4. **Post-brief activation nudge:** After a brief is generated, surface a one-click action: "Send this brief to your candidate." Captures the natural next step before the user leaves the browser tab.

---

### 5. White-Label Partners

**Current path to first value:**
Contact account lead > tier selection > branding configuration > cohort setup > participant invitations > first cohort active

**Time to first value:** 1 business day (branded environment live), 1 week (first cohort active)

**Friction points:**
- White-label setup currently requires human coordination with a Starting Monday account lead. This creates a 24-48 hour lag between intent and the ability to start configuring.
- Branding configuration (logo, colors, emails) is done through a settings page but there is no live preview of what the participant experience looks like. Partners are unsure if the branding looks right until a participant logs in.
- First-cohort setup overlaps with branding setup, creating confusion about sequencing (do I finish branding before I invite participants?).
- Role assignment for the partner's own team (firm admin, program manager, counselor) is not part of the default setup flow, so teams often operate with incorrect permissions for the first few weeks.

**Recommended improvements:**
1. **Self-serve tier selection and activation:** Allow partners to select their tier, enter billing, and activate without requiring a call. Gate advanced configuration (custom milestones, governance packs) behind the human onboarding, but let the basic environment activate immediately.
2. **Live branding preview:** On the branding settings screen, show a real-time preview of the participant-facing dashboard (including logo placement, primary color on navigation, and accent color on CTAs) as the partner edits settings. Eliminates the "does it look right?" uncertainty.
3. **Sequenced setup guide:** Add a visible setup checklist with 5 steps, ordered correctly: Branding > Track selection > Role assignment > Cohort creation > Participant invitations. Mark steps as complete and prevent skipping steps that have dependencies.
4. **Default role assignment:** During setup, require the partner to assign at least one firm admin and one program manager before the environment activates. This prevents the all-too-common situation where only one person has access.

---

## Universal Improvements (All Personas)

### In-product
1. **Progress indicator on signup.** Show new users how far they are from "useful." For executives: "Set up your target list to start receiving signals." For coaches: "Invite your first client to activate the session snapshot."
2. **Empty state coaching.** Every empty state (no companies in your list, no clients yet, no briefs generated) should have a single, specific, action-oriented call to action - not a generic "get started" message.
3. **First value notification.** The first time a meaningful event occurs for each persona (executive: first signal, coach: client takes action, outplacement: first participant activates), send a mobile push and email notification that names the specific event and suggests the next action.
4. **Value recap at day 7.** On day 7, send a personalized summary to each user: what they set up, what happened, and what to do next. Make "week 1 done" feel like a real milestone.

### Marketing site
1. **Persona-specific home page variation.** When a visitor arrives from a Google search for "executive coach tools" vs "outplacement software," serve a dynamically tailored hero headline and feature emphasis. The platform already has the persona routing infrastructure.
2. **Video walkthroughs.** Each persona landing page should have a 90-second screen recording showing the exact 3-click path from signup to first value moment. Text-based feature descriptions require interpretation; video requires none.
3. **Social proof placement.** Move verifiable proof (signal timing model, live demo) above the fold on every persona page. They are currently visible but not prominent enough to serve as the first thing a skeptical visitor sees.

---

## Priority Matrix

| Improvement | Persona | Effort | Impact | Priority |
|-------------|---------|--------|--------|----------|
| Auto-enable daily briefing | Executives | Low | High | 1 |
| Self-serve interactive preview | Coaches | Medium | High | 2 |
| Try-before-you-commit brief generator | Search firms | Medium | High | 3 |
| Starter company list on setup | Executives | Medium | High | 4 |
| Live branding preview | White label | Low | High | 5 |
| Counselor-first onboarding path | Outplacement | Medium | High | 6 |
| Guided cohort setup wizard | Outplacement | Medium | Medium | 7 |
| Lighter partner enrollment | Coaches | Low | Medium | 8 |
| Self-serve pilot calculator | Outplacement | Medium | Medium | 9 |
| Week-2 coach check-in automation | Coaches | Low | Medium | 10 |

This matrix should be reviewed monthly against actual activation and conversion metrics so priorities stay tied to measured outcomes.
